---
title: "Methods"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

## Core Components

::: {.version-grid}
::: {.method-card}
### CNO Baseline
The deterministic branch predicts a high-resolution correction from upsampled LR fields.
It gives the mean structure used as conditioning for the generative residual models.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### Flow Matching
The FM branch learns a velocity field between noise and the HR residual.
Inference integrates the learned velocity field with Euler or Heun solvers.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### Re-coarsening Consistency
The generated HR field is pooled back to LR resolution and penalized if it disagrees with the LR input.
This follows the consistency idea used in weather and ocean downscaling work.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### Classifier-Free Guidance
Condition dropout during training allows inference to combine conditional and unconditional velocities.
It is used to increase how strongly the model follows the CNO/LR conditioning.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### Swin Attention
Windowed attention targets local ocean structures such as fronts and eddies while keeping memory manageable.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### Pixel DiT
The DiT replaces convolutional bottlenecks with global token attention in pixel space.
It tests whether global spatial interaction improves residual energy placement.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### EDM / SongUNet
The v9a candidate uses a Song/NCSN++-style U-Net with EDM denoising, inspired by CorrDiff-style downscaling.
:::

::: {.method-card}
### Diffusion Bridge
The v9b candidate starts from a noisy CNO field instead of pure Gaussian noise, then learns a bridge toward HR.
:::
:::
